product.title: RŌZ Foundation Shampoo For All Hair Types & Textures | Hydrating & Cleansing For Healthy Hair| Vegan, Color-Safe, Free of Silicone, Sulfates, Paraben, SLS | Strengthens & Nourishes 10.1 oz/300 mL
featureBullets: GENTLE & THOROUGHLY CLEANSING: This hydrating, ph balancing shampoo creates the perfect foundation for air drying and styling. Coconut-based surfactants gently remove buildup while enhancing the hair’s natural movement.

BOTANICOMPLEX FOR STRONGER HAIR: Our proprietary complex restores hair health, strengthens strands, soothes the scalp, and preserves color. Powered by Grape Extract, Frankincense, Walnut Oil, and Ginger Root for nourishment.

HYDRATING & COLOR-SAFE: Rich lather cleanses without stripping, leaving hair soft, healthy, and primed for effortless styling. pH-balancing formula preserves color and scalp health while deeply hydrating.

VEGAN & FREE FROM HARSH INGREDIENTS: Made without silicones, parabens, sulfates, SLS, triclosan, or gluten. Cruelty-free and safe for all hair types and textures, ensuring a clean, high-performance cleanse.
LIGHTWEIGHT, BALANCED FORMULA: Strengthens, reinforces hair health, and promotes a hydrated scalp. Perfect for daily use, this award-winning shampoo delivers weightless nourishment without buildup.

specification.key: Other Special Features of the Product

reviewsInfo.customersSay: Customers find this shampoo effective and appreciate its delightful scent. The product makes hair feel clean and soft, with one customer noting it works well for fine hair. While some customers consider it well worth the money, others find it a bit pricey.

reviews.reviewText: This shampoo is our absolute favorite. It cleans my hair really well and leaves it so soft. The scent is intoxicated! Doesn't harm my color at all. When I saw that they had a refill, I jumped on it. I believe its about 2 and a half refills which has lasted us months!
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on December 15, 2025

reviews.reviewText: It was a little bit expensive but well worth it
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on January 30, 2026

reviews.reviewText: I have been struggling to find a new shampoo that will work with my hair now that it is changing as I age. I am approaching 34 and in perimenopause. My hair is color treated and very coarse and thick. I don’t know why there are complaints about the scent. I found the scent to be delightful and light. It was not strong at all. Perhaps some folks have more sensitive olfactory senses. I do not have sensitivities to scents so this shampoo smelled great to me. The lather was quick and left my hair feeling very clean and healthy. I am very satisfied with this shampoo. Pricey for the size, but honestly worth it.
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2025

reviews.reviewText: AMAZING SHAMPOO!!! My favorite in my shower. It smells amazing and really works. I love using all the Roz products together they are incredible. My hair feels so clean and healthy after I use this. Will never ever stop using.
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on June 19, 2025

reviews.reviewText: liked the shampoo but its dedinately not for wavy dry type hair or scalp. Left my hair and scalp super dry
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026

reviews.reviewText: The pump on this bottle was impossible to use. Could not get it to open despite the helpful hints provided by the company. The product also arrived leaking and all over the inside of the box. It smells nice. Please consider a bottle design that doesn’t require helpful hints about opening. Washing one’s hair should be a no brainer!!
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on February 28, 2026

reviews.reviewTitle: Roz Foundation Shampoo For All Hair Types & Textures
reviews.reviewText: LOVE this shampoo. My hair is very dry and coarse and haven't found right shampoo until now. This shampoo is a little pricey but well worth it. My hair feels soft and very manageable. Please try it, you won't be disappointed.
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2026
